title: The Overstory

features.name: the standing eucalyptus
features.desc-on-touch: a single tree you can walk beside, not inside. This one has not begun to count against itself. It remains whole the way the ravine below cannot be.

features.name: the survey markers
features.desc-on-touch: metal plaques driven into earth. Each one marks the same boundary from different years. The boundary has not moved, but the meaning has.

features.name: the maintenance shed
features.desc-on-touch: weathered boards. Inside, records of which trees were marked for removal and which were left. The lists conflict. Some trees on both.

features.name: the unfinished clearing
features.desc-on-touch: a space where growth was interrupted. Saplings now compete with the stumps they grew from. The clearing never completed itself. The forest never decided to close.

inscription: here the separate counts fall silent for a moment; the overstory doesn't add—it holds. You are between the ravine's division and the canopy's unity

file.name: Forest_Survey_1987_1989_2003_2014_Compilation_Read_As_Oath.pdf
file.note: forestry records layered transparently: same quadrants assessed across decades, each assessment refusing to complete the assessment before. A covenant written in unfinished sentences.

exits.label: back down into the ravine where the trees sing their own counts
deeper into the canopy where individual trees begin to recover the unity
along the ridge toward older eucalyptus stands that have kept silent records
